core_mechanism: encoding a scalar time-series as the potential of a linear self-adjoint (Schrödinger) eigenvalue operator over a sliding window, then using localization of its lowest eigenstate together with a sharp spike/divergence in the smallest eigenvalue's rate of change as an early-warning precursor of an approaching extreme event
brief_reason: The abstract constructs a Schrödinger equation whose potential is shaped by the signal within a moving window and reports Anderson localisation of the mode plus a reliably spiking numerical gradient of the minimum eigenvalue at the onset of extreme peaks.
